attempts.insight: The single Mathlib consumer of `Differential.ContainConstants` (`IsLiouville.trans` at `Mathlib/FieldTheory/Differential/Liouville.lean` L56) uses the class only on Liouville fields (`F → K` with `K/F` essentially-finite), not on standard-smooth k-algebras. Mathlib has the class but no instance for the project's standard-smooth case; this is a Mathlib-PR-grade gap, not a thin in-tree wrapper. The lemma as stated is FALSE without additional hypotheses (in char p > 0 with `B = k[X]`, `b = X^p` has `D b = 0` but `b ∉ range`). β-core consumer `df_zero_factors_through_constant_on_chart` (L196) delegates to this KDM lemma; iter-149+ signature inflation on KDM propagates to β-core but β-core has no other in-tree consumer (verified by grep), so the inflation is structurally safe.
